title: Hopper Item Sorter
description: Automatically routes a chosen item into its own chest. The classic single-item sorter: an input hopper feeds a horizontal 'sorter' hopper that points into a chest; the sorter hopper is filled with 18 of a 'filter' item (one stack of 4 + one of the target item across the first slots, the standard 41-4-1 trick is overkill — the simple version uses 4 filler items + the target). A comparator reads the sorter hopper and, via a redstone line into the hopper underneath, keeps non-matching items flowing past while matching items drop into the chest. Chain multiple sorter modules side by side, one per item type, along a shared hopper line. Requires comparators, redstone dust, and one filler item per slot.

steps.title: Stone base and input hopper line
steps.explanation: Stone floor at y=0, x=0–4, z=0–1. Place a hopper at (0,1,0) facing east — items flow east. Add a second hopper at (1,1,0) facing east (the filter hopper). Add a third hopper at (2,1,0) facing east (overflow continues). The filter hopper at (1,1,0) is where you put 4 filler items + 1 target item to lock up 5 slots.

steps.title: Output chest and comparator signal lock
steps.explanation: Place a chest at (1,1,1) — items matched by the filter hopper drop into this chest. Place a comparator at (1,2,0) facing south (reads the filter hopper at (1,1,0), outputs south toward z=1). Place a redstone torch at (1,0,1) on the side of the stone to always-power the filter hopper (keeping it locked). The comparator output at (1,2,1) controls whether the hopper is disabled — when the filter item is present, comparator output is low, hopper stays enabled. Chain more modules at x=3, x=5, etc. for additional item types.
